title: Gold in the rubble
productionNotes: Open with warm double bass walk and vintage soul sample (chopped vocal hum). Layer one-drop drums with deliberate, unhurried skank guitar on the offbeat. Introduce Hammond organ swells in pre-chorus, building emotional lift. Horn section (trumpet and trombone) punctuates chorus with triumphant stabs. Keep verses intimate with stripped instrumentation, then explode the chorus with full warmth—strings, backing vocals, melodica countermelodies. Bridge breaks to acoustic guitar and handclaps before final chorus elevation.
intro:
Chopped soul vocal sample hums over bass walk (8 bars)
Melodica plays contemplative melody
Drums enter bar 5 with gentle one-drop
verse1:
Dem tear down di temple, scatter every stone
Leave me inna di darkness, scrolling all alone
Every image burnin', every word a chain
But deep beneath di ashes, somethin' still remain

Mi dig through di filters, through di fake and lie
Past di broken mirror where mi used to cry
Through di comment section, through di endless scroll
Down to di foundation—dat's where mi find mi soul
prechorus:
Jah never build we cheap, no
We more than what dem see, oh
Di fire coulda rage but it cyan't burn di truth
Gold nah lose its value buried under roof
chorus:
Gold inna di rubble, precious inna di dust
Everything dem crumble still cyan't conquer us
Shine through di struggle, worth more than dem know
Gold inna di rubble, watch di real ting glow
Gold inna di rubble, yeah, gold inna di rubble

Mi shine, mi shine, mi shine through di rubble
Mi rise, mi rise, no more inna di trouble
Gold, gold, pure gold inna di rubble
Real recognize real, yeah, dat nah fi subtle
verse2:
Babylon sell illusion, package up and bright
Tell we seh we broken if we don't look right
But mi grandmother tell mi, wisdom inna she eye
'Pickney, yuh birth wid treasure money cyan't buy'

So mi brush off di rubble from mi heart and mind
Every scar a story, every wound refined
Dem did want mi polished, molded inna dem image
But mi worth was written long before di scrimmage
bridge:
Dig deep, dig deep, underneath di pain
Dig deep, dig deep, where di light remain
No algorithm measure what Jah put inside
Yuh value nah decrease when di world deny

Every soul a diamond, every heart a mine
Worth nah come from followers or dem dotted line
From di dust we rise, yeah, from di dust we free
Gold was always golden—now mi finally see
outro:
Melodica and horn fade with soul sample
Bass walks out with meditative resolution
Handclaps echo into silence
Final whispered ad-lib: 'Know yuhself... yuh worth more dan gold'
promptForSuno: Roots reggae soul fusion, 88 BPM, deep warm Jamaican baritone male vocals with authentic patois, one-drop drums, vintage soul vocal samples chopped and hummed, walking double bass, skank guitar offbeat, Hammond organ swells, triumphant horn section stabs, lush string arrangement, melodica countermelodies, hopeful and uplifting tone, earned optimism, spiritual excavation theme, warm analog production, 1970s roots reggae meets neo-soul, handclaps and backing vocals on bridge, rich harmonic layers, Bb major key, conscious lyrics about self-worth beyond digital validation
